text.title: Streaming: text_stream, Events, Async
text.summary: Three streaming patterns: text_stream for simple output, event-based for tools and fine-grained control, async streaming for concurrent pipelines; SSE event format reference.

example.code: with client.messages.stream( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a poem about AI"}] ) as stream: for text in stream.text_stream: print(text, end="", flush=True) # Get usage stats after streaming completes final = stream.get_final_message() print(f"\nTokens: {final.usage.input_tokens} in, {final.usage.output_tokens} out")
example.note: Use stream.get_final_message() when usage stats are needed — they are not available from individual chunks.

rule.statement: For streaming + tool use, accumulate input_json_delta events and parse after content_block_stop — never parse partial JSON inline.
rule.rationale: input_json_delta events carry fragments of JSON that are not individually parseable; parsing before the block stops raises JSONDecodeError.

example.code:
import asyncio import anthropic
async_client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()
async def stream_response(): async with async_client.messages.stream( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}] ) as stream: async for text in stream.text_stream: print(text, end="", flush=True)
asyncio.run(stream_response())

section.title: SSE event format
section.p: Raw SSE format for custom transport implementations:

list.item: message_start: message metadata (id, model, usage placeholder)
content_block_start: new content block begins (type: text or tool_use)

rule.statement: Claude SSE format differs from OpenAI SSE — they are not interchangeable without an adapter layer (e.g., LiteLLM).
rule.rationale: OpenAI uses data: [DONE] to end a stream; Claude uses message_stop event. The delta object structure also differs; treating them as equivalent causes silent parse failures.
